name: Chocolate-Dipped Biscotti
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Unsalted butter; melted and cooled
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Diane Mott Davidson
Preparation Time: 0:40
1. In a large mixing bowl, stir together the sugar and melted butter.
2. Add liqueur, whiskey, and anise seed. Beat in eggs, then stir in the nuts.
3. Sift the dry ingredients together until well incorporated. Cover with plastic wrap and chill for about 3 hours.
4. Set oven to 375°F. Butter 2 cookie sheets.
5. Shape the dough into 3 loaves on cookie sheets, well spaced. Each loaf should be about 2 inches wide and 1/2 inch thick.
6. Bake for about 20 minutes, until the loaves are puffed and browned.
7. When the loaves are cool enough to touch, cut each into diagonal slices about 1/2 inch thick.
8. Lay the slices on their cut sides and toast them at 375°F for an additional 15 minutes, or until lightly browned. Cool.
9. Melt chocolate chips with shortening in a double boiler, stirring frequently until a candy thermometer reads 85°F.
10. Holding each cookie by its bottom, gently dip the tops into chocolate.
11. Turn immediately and allow to dry, uncoated side down, on wax paper. Continue until all biscotti are topped with chocolate.
